summary: Defined and tested a deny-by-default canonical queue materialization authority contract that binds exact source and candidate digests, actor, workflow, path, operation, and expiry while remaining non-mutating.
observed: Authority is denied unless every explicit identity, binding, freshness, path, and candidate-state condition passes.
The evaluator never mutates canonical state.
Five deterministic Node tests were committed but not observed executing.
inferred: Snapshot consistency, reconciliation validity, and mutation authority are now represented as three separate layers.
proposed: Bind the actor and workflow identities to protected GitHub execution or a signed attestation before adding a mutation executor.
superseded: A reconciled candidate may authorize its own canonical materialization.
unresolved: Actor and workflow strings are policy identifiers, not yet cryptographically authenticated.
privacy_boundary: Public repository artifacts only; no raw private chat, health data, credentials, identifying personal information, or unreviewed uploads.
next_action: Run the committed authority tests in CI, bind policy identities to protected execution provenance, then implement a minimal exact-byte mutation executor with post-commit evidence.
